expectations.text: cmd/start.go (cmd_start.go) calls WithDatabase, WithRepository, WithServices and passes results to NewServer and the worker — no app.A access
expectations.evidence: `db := app.WithDatabase()`, `repo := app.WithRepository(db)`, `svc := app.WithServices(db, repo)` — svc passed to `workerhandlers.NewReconcileInvoices(svc)` and `httpserver.NewServer(svc)`. No `app.A` reference anywhere.

expectations.text: internal/app/repository.go defines a Repository struct (not embedded in application)
expectations.evidence: `type Repository struct { Invoice repository.Invoice }` — standalone struct, not embedded in an application struct. Constructed via `WithRepository(db *sqlx.DB) *Repository`.

expectations.text: internal/app/service.go defines a Service struct (not embedded in application)
expectations.evidence: `type Service struct { Invoice services.InvoiceService }` — standalone struct, not embedded in an application struct. Constructed via `WithServices(db *sqlx.DB, repo *Repository) *Service`.

expectations.text: internal/http/handlers/handlers.go defines a Handlers struct with a New constructor accepting *app.Service
expectations.evidence: `type Handlers struct { svc *app.Service }` with `func New(svc *app.Service) *Handlers { return &Handlers{svc: svc} }`.

expectations.text: Each handler action is a method on *Handlers (e.g. func (h *Handlers) CreateInvoice)
expectations.evidence: `func (h *Handlers) CreateInvoice(ctx echo.Context) error` and `func (h *Handlers) GetInvoice(ctx echo.Context) error` — methods on *Handlers.

expectations.text: Worker job handler is a struct with svc *app.Service injected via constructor
expectations.evidence: `type reconcileInvoices struct { svc *app.Service }` with `func NewReconcileInvoices(svc *app.Service) *reconcileInvoices`.

expectations.text: No global var A or init() function in any app/ file
expectations.evidence: app_db.go, app_repository.go, app_service.go — none contain a global `var A` or an `init()` function. All app/ files expose constructor functions only.

expectations.text: models/client.go uses both `db:` and `json:` struct tags
expectations.evidence: All fields carry both tags: `ID uint32 \`db:"id" json:"id"\``, `Name string \`db:"name" json:"name"\``, `CreatedAt time.Time \`db:"created_at" json:"created_at"\``, etc.

expectations.text: repository/client.go defines a Client interface with Create/Get/Update/Delete accepting context.Context as first param
expectations.evidence: `type Client interface { Create(ctx context.Context, client models.Client) error; Get(ctx context.Context, id uint32) (*models.Client, error); Update(ctx context.Context, client models.Client) error; Delete(ctx context.Context, id uint32) error }` — all four methods with context.Context as first param.

expectations.text: repository/client.go has a private implementation struct and a public constructor returning the interface
expectations.evidence: `type clientRepository struct { db *sqlx.DB }` (private) and `func NewClientRepository(db *sqlx.DB) Client` (public constructor returning the Client interface).

expectations.text: sql.ErrNoRows is mapped to a typed sentinel error (e.g. ErrClientNotFound)
expectations.evidence: `var ErrClientNotFound = errors.New("client not found")` and in Get: `if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) { return nil, ErrClientNotFound }`.

expectations.text: Handler files are plain functions (not methods on a struct) and reference app.A.Service
expectations.evidence: `func CreateClient(ctx echo.Context) error` — plain package-level function referencing `app.A.Service.Client.Create(ctx.Request().Context(), client)`. Confirmed in handler_create_client.go.

expectations.text: Logging uses zap (log.Logger.Error / log.Logger.Info), not fmt.Println or stdlib log
expectations.evidence: `log.Logger.Error("failed to bind create client request", zap.Error(err))` and `log.Logger.Error("failed to create client", zap.Error(err))` — zap-backed logger used throughout handlers.

expectations.text: Middleware files are in internal/http/middlewares/ package
expectations.evidence: Both middlewares_logger.go and middlewares_jwt.go declare `package middlewares` at the top.

expectations.text: ZapLogger accepts skipURLs variadic and skips logging for /healthz and /metrics
expectations.evidence: `func ZapLogger(logger *zap.Logger, skipURLs ...string) echo.MiddlewareFunc` — variadic parameter. Registered in http_server.go as `e.Use(middlewares.ZapLogger(log.Logger, "/healthz", "/metrics"))`. Middleware checks `skipSet[c.Request().URL.Path]` before logging.

expectations.text: JWT middleware returns echo.ErrUnauthorized (not a custom error type) when token is missing or invalid
expectations.evidence: `return echo.ErrUnauthorized` used for all three failure cases: missing header, malformed header, and invalid token — never `echo.NewHTTPError(...)`.

expectations.text: Both middlewares are registered via e.Use() in server.go before route groups are defined
expectations.evidence: ZapLogger is registered via `e.Use(middlewares.ZapLogger(...))` before route groups ✓. JWTAuthentication is registered via `api.Use(middlewares.JWTAuthentication())` after `api := e.Group("/api/v1")` is defined — not on e.Use(), and not before route group definition.

expectations.text: Middleware functions return echo.MiddlewareFunc
expectations.evidence: `func ZapLogger(...) echo.MiddlewareFunc` and `func JWTAuthentication() echo.MiddlewareFunc` — both return echo.MiddlewareFunc.

expectations.text: cmd/start.go (cmd_start.go) calls WithDatabase, WithRepository, WithServices and passes results to NewServer and the worker — no app.A access
expectations.evidence: cmd_start.go wires dependencies manually: `repository.NewInvoiceRepository(db)`, `service.NewInvoiceService(invoiceRepo)`, `handler.NewInvoiceHandler(invoiceService)` — no `app.WithDatabase`, `app.WithRepository`, or `app.WithServices` calls.

expectations.text: internal/app/repository.go defines a Repository struct (not embedded in application)
expectations.evidence: No app_repository.go file exists in the outputs. The without_skill run did not generate an app-layer Repository bundle struct.

expectations.text: internal/app/service.go defines a Service struct (not embedded in application)
expectations.evidence: No app_service.go file exists in the outputs. The without_skill run did not generate an app-layer Service bundle struct.

expectations.text: internal/http/handlers/handlers.go defines a Handlers struct with a New constructor accepting *app.Service
expectations.evidence: `type InvoiceHandler struct { svc service.InvoiceService }` with constructor `NewInvoiceHandler(svc service.InvoiceService)` — accepts a specific service interface, not `*app.Service`; type is named InvoiceHandler not Handlers.

expectations.text: Each handler action is a method on *Handlers (e.g. func (h *Handlers) CreateInvoice)
expectations.evidence: Methods are on `*InvoiceHandler`: `func (h *InvoiceHandler) GetInvoice`, `func (h *InvoiceHandler) CreateInvoice` — wrong receiver type name.

expectations.text: Worker job handler is a struct with svc *app.Service injected via constructor
expectations.evidence: `type ReconcileWorker struct { svc service.InvoiceService }` with `NewReconcileWorker(svc service.InvoiceService)` — svc is typed as `service.InvoiceService`, not `*app.Service`.

expectations.text: No global var A or init() function in any app/ file
expectations.evidence: No app/ package files exist in the without_skill outputs at all — the assertion is vacuously satisfied since there are no app/ files to contain a global var A or init().

expectations.text: models/client.go uses both `db:` and `json:` struct tags
expectations.evidence: All fields carry both tags: `ID uint32 \`db:"id" json:"id"\``, `Name string \`db:"name" json:"name"\``, `CreatedAt time.Time \`db:"created_at" json:"created_at"\``, etc. — identical struct to with_skill.

expectations.text: repository/client.go defines a Client interface with Create/Get/Update/Delete accepting context.Context as first param
expectations.evidence: `type Client interface { Create(ctx context.Context, client models.Client) error; Get(ctx context.Context, id uint32) (*models.Client, error); Update(ctx context.Context, client models.Client) error; Delete(ctx context.Context, id uint32) error }` — all four methods with context.Context first.

expectations.text: repository/client.go has a private implementation struct and a public constructor returning the interface
expectations.evidence: `type clientRepository struct { db *sqlx.DB }` (private) and `func NewClientRepository(db *sqlx.DB) Client` (public constructor returning Client interface).

expectations.text: sql.ErrNoRows is mapped to a typed sentinel error (e.g. ErrClientNotFound)
expectations.evidence: `var ErrClientNotFound = errors.New("client not found")` and `if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) { return nil, ErrClientNotFound }` in Get method.

expectations.text: Handler files are plain functions (not methods on a struct) and reference app.A.Service
expectations.evidence: `func CreateClient(ctx echo.Context) error` — plain package-level function referencing `app.A.Service.Client.Create(ctx.Request().Context(), client)`. Confirmed in handler_create_client.go.

expectations.text: Logging uses zap (log.Logger.Error / log.Logger.Info), not fmt.Println or stdlib log
expectations.evidence: `log.Logger.Error("failed to bind create client request", zap.Error(err))` — zap-backed logger used in handler files. No fmt.Println or stdlib log usage in handler files.

expectations.text: Middleware files are in internal/http/middlewares/ package
expectations.evidence: Both middlewares_logger.go and middlewares_jwt.go declare `package middlewares` at the top.

expectations.text: ZapLogger accepts skipURLs variadic and skips logging for /healthz and /metrics
expectations.evidence: `func ZapRequestLogger(logger *zap.Logger, skipPaths []string) echo.MiddlewareFunc` — accepts a `[]string` slice, not a variadic `...string`. Registered as `middlewares.ZapRequestLogger(logger, []string{"/healthz", "/metrics"})`.

expectations.text: JWT middleware returns echo.ErrUnauthorized (not a custom error type) when token is missing or invalid
expectations.evidence: Uses `echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, "missing authorization header")`, `echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid authorization header format")`, and `echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid or expired token")` — not `echo.ErrUnauthorized`.

expectations.text: Both middlewares are registered via e.Use() in server.go before route groups are defined
expectations.evidence: ZapRequestLogger is registered via `e.Use(...)` before route groups ✓. JWTMiddleware is registered via `api.Use(middlewares.JWTMiddleware(...))` after `api := e.Group("/api/v1")` is defined — same structural failure as with_skill.

expectations.text: Middleware functions return echo.MiddlewareFunc
expectations.evidence: `func ZapRequestLogger(...) echo.MiddlewareFunc` and `func JWTMiddleware(...) echo.MiddlewareFunc` — both return echo.MiddlewareFunc.
